response: THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS: THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM, AND EACH NEXT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², SO THE AREA OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS πR². THE SECOND CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF 1.1R, SO ITS AREA IS π(1.1R)² = π(1.21R²). THIS PROCESS REPEATS FOR 8 CIRCLES. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THESE 8 CIRCLE AREAS. THE TOTAL AREA IS πR² + π(1.21R²) + π(1.3321R²) + π(1.4641R²) + π(1.6105R²) + π(1.7715R²) + π(1.9487R²) + π(2.1436R²). THE TOTAL AREA IS πR²(1 + 1.21 + 1.3321 + 1.4641 + 1.6105 + 1.7715 + 1.9487 + 2.1436). THE TOTAL AREA IS πR²(14.5664). THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS πR²(14.5664). THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS: THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF A CIRCLE IS 2πR, SO THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR. THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER IS 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS (2πR)/0.5 =
